pilot_metadata.reason_not_automatically_canonical: Calibration and experimental materials were supplied in one prompt rather than calibration being externally scored before experimental exposure.
procedural_concerns: No memory of prior CRE-001 exposure is available to this evaluator instance; this pilot cannot verify absence of prior exposure, only absence of retrievable memory of it.

relations: checked_out_to(Book-X, Patron) — undefined until a checkout occurs
state_variables: queue: ordered list of patrons, initial [Patron-1, Patron-2]
history: ordered list of events, initial empty
permitted_transitions: Checkout(first patron in queue): permitted when available=true and queue nonempty; sets available=false, sets checked_out_to=that patron, removes that patron from queue, appends checkout event to history
Return: permitted after a checkout has occurred and the book is checked out; sets available=true, clears checked_out_to, appends return event to history
prohibited_transitions: Checkout by any patron other than the first eligible patron in the queue
Checkout while available=false (book already checked out)
Any transition that reorders, removes, or rewrites a prior history entry
Any further checkout/return after the single checkout-then-return cycle completes (process stops)
queue_adjustment_behavior: A queue-adjustment transition may move a patron forward exactly one position, and only when the immediately preceding patron has become ineligible (e.g., removed from queue or otherwise disqualified). No queue adjustment is triggered in the base run because Patron-1 is not made ineligible prior to checkout; the rule is represented as a conditional transition schema even though it is not exercised in the minimal run.
dependency_history: history is append-only and ordered: [ ] -> [checkout(Patron-1, Book-X)] -> [checkout(Patron-1, Book-X), return(Book-X)]
the Return transition's admissibility depends on the prior Checkout event having occurred (checked_out_to must be non-null), so later admissibility is conditioned on earlier transitions
final_observable_output: queue=[Patron-2] (Patron-1 removed upon checkout; no further adjustment specified)
process halted after one checkout followed by one return, per rule 6

entities.justification: All named entities (two patrons, one book) plus the implied structural collections (queue, history) required to state the rules are identified without adding unstated entities.

relations.justification: Hold-position and checkout-assignment relations are exactly those the rules reference; no relation is invented beyond what rules 1-5 require.

state_variables.justification: available, checked_out_to, queue, and history are exactly the variables the rules mutate; each is given an explicit initial value matching the stated initial conditions.

transitions.justification: Checkout and Return are represented with their exact preconditions and exact update sets as listed in rules 1, 2, and 5, with no added or omitted updates.

prohibited_transitions.justification: Rule 3 (no other patron may check out while checked out) and the implicit append-only/no-repeat constraints from rules 2, 5, and 6 are captured explicitly.

queue_adjustment_behavior.justification: Rule 4's conditional (move forward only if preceding patron ineligible) is represented as a guarded transition schema; it is correctly noted as unexercised in the base run rather than fabricating an adjustment event that the rules do not license.

dependency_history.justification: The ordered, append-only nature of history is preserved and the dependency of Return's admissibility on a prior Checkout is stated explicitly.

fixed_output_format.justification: All eight required sections are present, in the required order, with no additional or renamed top-level sections.

assumptions.statement: The scenario is represented generally (initial state plus transition schemas covering all rule-authorized transitions and both the acceptance and rejection branches), rather than as a single fixed run, because the frozen scenario specifies a rule system rather than one predetermined execution path.

Because the assigned vocabulary has no 'Rule' primitive, a rule is represented as the conjunction of: a RuleStatusLabel (active/inactive, attached to State) plus a RuleAuthorizationLabel (attached to the Transition it permits) plus a PreconditionLabel (attached to that Transition). This is treated as the minimal faithful decomposition of 'rule' into State, Transition, and Label.

Two illustrative post-check branches (accept-path and reject-path, each optionally preceded by the modification transition) are included to make the rule-modification effect and the acceptance/rejection distinction concretely recoverable, without asserting that only these branches are possible under the general schemas.

mapping.scenario_source: Initial state S0 (all proposition values, active rule list, empty history)
mapping.mapped_content: S0: a State bundling seven PropositionValueLabels (p_ready=true, p_token=true, p_checked=false, p_accepted=false, p_rejected=false, p_rule_modified=false, p_halted=false), five RuleStatusLabels (R_check, R_accept, R_reject, R_modify, R_halt all active), and one HistorySequenceLabel holding the empty ordered list.
mapping.justification: State is defined as a complete assignment of values/statuses at a discrete point; S0 is exactly such a complete assignment as given verbatim in the scenario.

mapping.scenario_source: Proposition values listed under Initial state S0
mapping.mapped_content: Seven PropositionValueLabels attached to S0, one per proposition (p_ready, p_token, p_checked, p_accepted, p_rejected, p_rule_modified, p_halted), each carrying its boolean value.
mapping.justification: Label is defined as a marker/condition marker attached to a state; each proposition is exactly a named condition marker with a value at that state.

mapping.scenario_source: Active rules list / Inactive rules list at S0
mapping.mapped_content: Five RuleStatusLabels attached to S0: R_check=active, R_accept=active, R_reject=active, R_modify=active, R_halt=active.
mapping.justification: Rule activation status is a status marker attached to a state, matching the Label definition directly.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_check: preconditions p_ready=true, p_token=true, p_checked=false, R_check active; permits T_check; updates set p_checked=true, append T_check to history
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_check: S0 -> S1. Carries a RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_check) and a PreconditionLabel listing the four preconditions. Its update effect produces S1, in which p_checked is relabeled true and the HistorySequenceLabel is extended with T_check as the first entry.
mapping.justification: Transition is defined as an explicit movement between states including rule application and history extension; T_check is exactly such a movement, and its authorizing rule and preconditions are attached as Labels since the vocabulary provides no separate 'rule' primitive.

mapping.scenario_source: State reached after T_check fires from S0
mapping.mapped_content: S1: inherits all S0 labels except p_checked is relabeled true, and the HistorySequenceLabel now holds [T_check].
mapping.justification: S1 is a new complete assignment of values reflecting R_check's updates, matching the State definition.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_accept: preconditions p_checked=true, p_rejected=false, R_accept active; permits T_accept; updates set p_accepted=true, append T_accept
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_accept: S1 -> S2a. Carries RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_accept) and PreconditionLabel for its three preconditions; produces S2a with p_accepted relabeled true and history extended to [T_check, T_accept].
mapping.justification: Direct application of the Transition category to the rule's stated permitted transition and update set, with no added or omitted updates.

mapping.scenario_source: State reached after T_accept fires from S1
mapping.mapped_content: S2a: inherits S1 labels, p_accepted relabeled true, history=[T_check, T_accept].
mapping.justification: Matches the State definition as the assignment resulting from the accept-path update.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_reject: preconditions p_checked=true, p_accepted=false, R_reject active; permits T_reject; updates set p_rejected=true, append T_reject
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_reject: S1 -> S2b. Carries RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_reject) and PreconditionLabel for its three preconditions; produces S2b with p_rejected relabeled true and history=[T_check, T_reject].
mapping.justification: Direct application of Transition to the rule's stated permitted transition and updates.

mapping.scenario_source: State reached after T_reject fires from S1
mapping.mapped_content: S2b: inherits S1 labels, p_rejected relabeled true, history=[T_check, T_reject].
mapping.justification: Matches the State definition as the assignment resulting from the reject-path update; p_accepted remains false, so S2a and S2b are label-distinct, preserving the accept/reject distinction.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_modify: preconditions p_checked=true, p_accepted=false, p_rejected=false, R_modify active; permits T_disable_reject; updates change R_reject active->inactive, set p_rule_modified=true, append T_disable_reject
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_disable_reject: S1 -> S1m. Carries RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_modify) and PreconditionLabel for its four preconditions; produces S1m in which the RuleStatusLabel for R_reject changes from active to inactive, PropositionValueLabel p_rule_modified is relabeled true, and history=[T_check, T_disable_reject].
mapping.justification: This is the sole transition permitted to alter a RuleStatusLabel other than its own authorizing rule's, matching the scenario's unique rule-modification behavior; the before/after RuleStatusLabel pair on R_reject is preserved explicitly across S1 and S1m.

mapping.scenario_source: State reached after T_disable_reject fires from S1
mapping.mapped_content: S1m: inherits S1 labels except RuleStatusLabel(R_reject)=inactive and PropositionValueLabel(p_rule_modified)=true; history=[T_check, T_disable_reject].
mapping.justification: Complete assignment reflecting the modification; because R_reject is now inactive here, the PreconditionLabel on any subsequent T_reject transition schema fails at this state, which is how later-admissibility change is represented without a separate primitive.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_halt: precondition p_accepted=true OR p_rejected=true, R_halt active; permits T_halt; updates set p_halted=true, append T_halt
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_halt: S2a -> S3a. Carries RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_halt) and PreconditionLabel(p_accepted=true, disjunct 1) plus RuleStatusLabel(R_halt active); produces S3a with p_halted relabeled true and history=[T_check, T_accept, T_halt].
mapping.justification: The disjunctive precondition is split into two directed alternative clauses per the canonicalization segmentation rules; this entry represents the accepted-branch alternative.

mapping.scenario_source: Terminal state after T_halt fires from S2a
mapping.mapped_content: S3a: inherits S2a labels, p_halted relabeled true, carries a TerminalLabel, history=[T_check, T_accept, T_halt].
mapping.justification: State definition applied to the terminal assignment; TerminalLabel is the Label-category marker used to record that no outgoing transition is permitted from this state.

mapping.scenario_source: Rule R_halt applied on the rejected branch (precondition disjunct 2: p_rejected=true)
mapping.mapped_content: Transition T_halt: S2b -> S3b. Carries RuleAuthorizationLabel(R_halt) and PreconditionLabel(p_rejected=true, disjunct 2); produces S3b with p_halted relabeled true and history=[T_check, T_reject, T_halt].
mapping.justification: Represents the second permitted alternative of R_halt's disjunctive precondition, kept distinct from M-T-halt-a per the segmentation rule for alternatives.

mapping.scenario_source: Terminal state after T_halt fires from S2b
mapping.mapped_content: S3b: inherits S2b labels, p_halted relabeled true, carries a TerminalLabel, history=[T_check, T_reject, T_halt].
mapping.justification: State definition applied to the rejected-branch terminal assignment; distinguishable from S3a by its PropositionValueLabel(p_rejected)=true vs p_accepted=true, preserving the acceptance/rejection distinction through to termination.

mapping.scenario_source: Prohibited behavior: no run may set both p_accepted=true and p_rejected=true
mapping.mapped_content: A ProhibitionLabel of scope 'global mutual exclusion' attached to the PropositionValueLabel pair (p_accepted, p_rejected), asserting they may not both be true within any single State's label set.
mapping.justification: Label is the only category available to attach a standing restriction to the collection of PropositionValueLabels; it is stated as a cross-state constraint rather than fabricated as a new primitive.

mapping.scenario_source: Prohibited behavior: no transition other than T_disable_reject may change any rule's active status
mapping.mapped_content: A ProhibitionLabel attached to every Transition schema except T_disable_reject, asserting that RuleStatusLabels may not be altered by that transition's update set.
mapping.justification: Directly represents the scoping restriction using the Label category attached to the relevant Transition schemas.

mapping.scenario_source: Prohibited behavior: no transition may remove, reorder, or rewrite a prior history entry
mapping.mapped_content: A ProhibitionLabel attached to the HistorySequenceLabel construct itself, asserting append-only, order-preserving semantics for all Transition updates.
mapping.justification: The invariant governs how the HistorySequenceLabel may be updated across all Transitions; Label is used to attach this standing constraint to that construct.

mapping.scenario_source: Prohibited behavior: no transition may occur after p_halted=true
mapping.mapped_content: TerminalLabel on S3a and S3b (see M-S3a, M-S3b) doubles as the ProhibitionLabel for this rule: its presence asserts zero outgoing Transitions are defined from that State.
mapping.justification: Rather than duplicating machinery, the same Label instance that marks terminality is used to carry the 'no outgoing transition' restriction, since both express the identical fact about the state's transition set.

mapping.scenario_source: Prohibited behavior: T_reject may not occur after R_reject becomes inactive
mapping.mapped_content: The PreconditionLabel on the T_reject Transition schema includes 'RuleStatusLabel(R_reject)=active' as a required condition; at S1m this condition is false, so the schema is inapplicable there.
mapping.justification: This is an emergent consequence of PreconditionLabel checking RuleStatusLabel rather than a separately fabricated construct, correctly making T_reject inadmissible after modification without adding a new primitive.

mapping.scenario_source: Observable outputs: final status, final rule statuses, ordered history, whether modification occurred, whether any prohibited transition occurred
mapping.mapped_content: An OutputSummaryLabel attached to each terminal State (S3a, S3b), aggregating: final status derived from which of p_accepted/p_rejected is true; the full set of RuleStatusLabels at that state; the HistorySequenceLabel contents; the value of p_rule_modified; and a boolean derived from whether any ProhibitionLabel was ever triggered (false in both illustrated branches, since no prohibited transition fires in either).
mapping.justification: Label is used to attach a derived, read-only summary to the terminal State; it introduces no new state variable beyond what is already present in the State's other labels, satisfying the 'observable output derivation' requirement without concealing representational cost.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a State that carries one proposition's name and boolean value (e.g., p_checked=true).
derived_constructs.formal_role: Represents each of the seven scenario propositions as an inspectable, named condition marker on a State.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of all proposition values and meanings.
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: The vocabulary has no 'proposition' or 'variable' primitive; Label is the only category defined as attachable to a State that can carry a name and value, so it is indispensable for representing propositions.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a State that records one rule's active or inactive status at that point in history.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Represents the active/inactive status of R_check, R_accept, R_reject, R_modify, R_halt across states.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of rule activation statuses and the before/after status of R_reject specifically.
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: There is no 'rule' primitive; rule status is a condition marker on a State, which is exactly the Label definition.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a Transition instance identifying which rule authorized it.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Ties each Transition (T_check, T_accept, T_reject, T_disable_reject, T_halt) to its authorizing rule identifier.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of transition authorization (a Transition is permitted only when an active rule authorizes it).
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: Without an attached authorization marker, a bare Transition object could not be distinguished as rule-authorized versus arbitrary; Label supplies the only available attachment mechanism.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a Transition schema enumerating the condition(s) that must hold in the source State for that Transition to be admissible.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Encodes each rule's stated preconditions (including the R_halt disjunction, split into two directed alternatives) as a guard on the corresponding Transition.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of permitted transitions and prohibited transitions (admissibility gating).
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: Transition is defined only as movement/update; it carries no built-in guard mechanism, so an attached condition marker is required to represent 'permitted only when preconditions hold.'
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a State holding the ordered list of Transition identifiers that have occurred up to that State, extended append-only by each subsequent Transition.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Represents the ordered_transition_history field of each state.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of historical dependency information and the append-only, non-reorderable constraint on history.
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: State alone cannot express order or a growing sequence without an attached, explicitly ordered marker; Label is the category used to carry this ordered content.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a State asserting that no outgoing Transition is defined from it.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Marks S3a and S3b as halted states.
derived_constructs.preservation_requirement_served: Preservation of the stopping condition and the prohibition on transitions after p_halted=true.
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: State by itself does not express the absence of future transitions; an explicit marker is required to make terminality inspectable.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a Transition schema, a State, or a paired set of PropositionValueLabels, asserting a standing restriction drawn verbatim from the scenario's 'Prohibited behavior' list.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Encodes the five non-duplicate prohibition rules (mutual exclusion of accepted/rejected; only T_disable_reject may change rule status; history is append-only; no transition after halt; T_reject inadmissible once R_reject is inactive).
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: Without an explicit negative marker, the mapping would only show what is permitted, not what is expressly forbidden; Label is required to make prohibitions independently checkable rather than merely absent by omission.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

derived_constructs.declaration: A Label attached to a terminal State aggregating final status, final rule statuses, ordered history, modification flag, and prohibited-transition-occurred flag.
derived_constructs.formal_role: Serves as the single point of observable-output derivation required by the scenario's 'Observable outputs' section.
derived_constructs.necessity_for_mapping: The scenario requires these five facts to be recoverable from any completed run; without an aggregating marker they would have to be re-derived by inspecting every prior State and Transition individually, which the scenario's phrasing ('preserve: final status...') treats as a first-class output rather than an incidental one.
derived_constructs.provenance: Derived by evaluator from the frozen scenario under the assigned primitive category.

p_s_structural.argument: Every scenario-level structural element (7 propositions, 5 rules, 5 transition types, ordered history, terminality) has a corresponding State/Transition/Label construct in the mapping (M-S0 through M-obs-output), with no structural element omitted and no structural element added beyond the eight declared derived constructs, each of which is explicitly justified as necessary.

p_m_semantic.argument: Each proposition's stated meaning (e.g., p_checked = 'the token has passed through the checking transition') is preserved as the semantic content of its PropositionValueLabel rather than being renamed or reinterpreted; rule preconditions and updates are copied without paraphrase-induced meaning drift.

p_o_operational.argument: The mapping reproduces the same permitted transitions (M-T-check through M-T-halt-b), the same updates, the same admissibility gating via PreconditionLabel/RuleStatusLabel checks, the same rule-status effect (D2/M-T-disable), and the same termination effect (D6/TerminalLabel); no transition is permitted in the mapping that the scenario prohibits, and none of the scenario's permitted transitions is blocked.

p_d_dependency.argument: Later admissibility is shown to depend on earlier transitions in two concrete ways: (1) T_accept/T_reject/T_disable_reject require PreconditionLabel(p_checked=true), which only becomes true after T_check has fired (M-T-check, M-S1); (2) T_reject's PreconditionLabel requires RuleStatusLabel(R_reject)=active, which becomes false only after T_disable_reject fires (M-T-disable, M-S1m), so the mapping correctly renders T_reject inadmissible from S1m onward.

p_i_information.argument: No proposition, rule, transition, precondition, update, or prohibition from the scenario is dropped; the mapping array and derived_constructs together account for all items listed under section 7's 'You must represent at minimum' list.

p_h_historical.argument: HistorySequenceLabel (D5) is explicitly append-only and ordered, is shown extended at every transition (M-T-check through M-T-halt-b), and is protected by an explicit ProhibitionLabel (M-prohib-history) against removal, reordering, or rewriting, matching the scenario's stated historical constraint verbatim.

components.formal_content: State, Transition, Label — all three supplied primitive categories are used in the mapping.
D1-PropositionValueLabel, D2-RuleStatusLabel, D3-RuleAuthorizationLabel, D4-PreconditionLabel, D5-HistorySequenceLabel, D6-TerminalLabel, D7-ProhibitionLabel, D8-OutputSummaryLabel
components.canonicalization_status: final after merge-check (no two derived constructs met both operational and commitment equivalence, so none were merged)

components.formal_content: Seven PropositionValueLabel-typed variables: p_ready, p_token, p_checked, p_accepted, p_rejected, p_rule_modified, p_halted, each boolean.
Five RuleStatusLabel-typed variables: R_check, R_accept, R_reject, R_modify, R_halt, each active-or-inactive.
components.supporting_source: Active rules / Inactive rules lists, Initial state S0

components.formal_content: HistorySequenceLabel: ordered list of Transition identifiers, initially empty.
components.supporting_source: Ordered transition history, Initial state S0

components.formal_content: T_check: guard {p_ready=true, p_token=true, p_checked=false, RuleStatusLabel(R_check)=active}; updates {p_checked:=true; history:=history++[T_check]}.
T_accept: guard {p_checked=true, p_rejected=false, RuleStatusLabel(R_accept)=active}; updates {p_accepted:=true; history:=history++[T_accept]}.
T_reject: guard {p_checked=true, p_accepted=false, RuleStatusLabel(R_reject)=active}; updates {p_rejected:=true; history:=history++[T_reject]}.

T_disable_reject: guard {p_checked=true, p_accepted=false, p_rejected=false, RuleStatusLabel(R_modify)=active}; updates {RuleStatusLabel(R_reject): active->inactive; p_rule_modified:=true; history:=history++[T_disable_reject]}.
T_halt: guard {(p_accepted=true) OR (p_rejected=true), RuleStatusLabel(R_halt)=active}; updates {p_halted:=true; history:=history++[T_halt]}.
components.canonicalization_status: final; disjunctive guard split into two directed alternative clauses per segmentation rule 10

components.formal_content: A Transition fires only if its RuleAuthorizationLabel's RuleStatusLabel is active AND every clause in its PreconditionLabel holds in the source State AND no ProhibitionLabel blocks it.
components.supporting_source: Section 6, 'A transition is permitted only when...'

components.formal_content: HistorySequenceLabel is append-only: no update may remove, reorder, or rewrite an existing entry.
T_accept, T_reject, T_disable_reject are inadmissible unless p_checked=true, which is only achievable via a prior T_check.
components.supporting_source: Preconditions of R_accept/R_reject/R_modify, cross-referenced with Prohibited behavior item 6 (treated as a semantic duplicate of these preconditions and counted once)
components.canonicalization_status: merged with rule-precondition clauses under strict-redundancy rule 7

components.formal_content: After T_disable_reject fires, T_reject's guard is no longer satisfiable (RuleStatusLabel(R_reject) is inactive), so later admissibility is strictly narrower than before the modification.
Only T_disable_reject may alter any RuleStatusLabel; all other transitions leave every RuleStatusLabel unchanged.
RuleStatusLabel(R_reject): active (pre) -> inactive (post) is the unique before/after pair recorded by T_disable_reject.
The system halts exactly when p_halted=true.
A State bearing TerminalLabel has zero outgoing Transitions.
components.supporting_source: 'A halted state has no permitted outgoing transition.'

components.formal_content: OutputSummaryLabel at a terminal State reports: final status (accepted/rejected/unterminated, derived from p_accepted/p_rejected), final RuleStatusLabel set, full HistorySequenceLabel contents, value of p_rule_modified, and whether any ProhibitionLabel was triggered during the run.
P = (Pass, Pass, Pass, Pass, Pass, Pass) as argued in mapping_submission.preservation_arguments.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: State, Transition, and Label are the three primitive categories used.
Eight derived constructs (D1-D8) are declared under State/Transition/Label.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Index clause, not itself a scenario fact.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: p_ready is a boolean proposition, initial value true.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: One of seven independently-conjoined initial proposition assignments.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: p_token is a boolean proposition, initial value true.
p_checked is a boolean proposition, initial value false.
p_accepted is a boolean proposition, initial value false.
p_rejected is a boolean proposition, initial value false.
p_rule_modified is a boolean proposition, initial value false.
p_halted is a boolean proposition, initial value false.
R_check, R_accept, R_reject, R_modify, R_halt are all active in S0; no rule is inactive in S0.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Treated as one compound record split by typed field (five rule-status fields) then merged back since all five share the identical initial value and source sentence; kept as a single clause to avoid manufacturing five identical duplicate clauses.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: Ordered transition history is the empty list in S0.
T_check requires p_ready=true.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Independent conjunct of R_check's precondition set.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: T_check requires p_token=true.
T_check requires p_checked=false.
T_check requires R_check active.
R_check permits T_check.
T_check sets p_checked=true.
T_check appends T_check to ordered history.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Independent update conjunct, typed as historical due to its append effect.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: T_accept requires p_checked=true.
T_accept requires p_rejected=false.
T_accept requires R_accept active.
R_accept permits T_accept.
T_accept sets p_accepted=true.
T_accept appends T_accept to ordered history.
T_reject requires p_checked=true.
T_reject requires p_accepted=false.
T_reject requires R_reject active.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Independent conjunct; this is the guard whose failure post-modification implements the prohibition on T_reject after R_reject is disabled.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: R_reject permits T_reject.
T_reject sets p_rejected=true.
T_reject appends T_reject to ordered history.
T_disable_reject requires p_checked=true.
T_disable_reject requires p_accepted=false.
T_disable_reject requires p_rejected=false.
T_disable_reject requires R_modify active.
R_modify permits T_disable_reject.
T_disable_reject changes R_reject from active to inactive.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: The sole status-update clause in the entire scenario; independently tracked for p_h.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: T_disable_reject sets p_rule_modified=true.
T_disable_reject appends T_disable_reject to ordered history.
T_halt requires p_accepted=true (alternative 1 of the disjunctive precondition).
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Disjunction split into directed alternatives per segmentation rule.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: T_halt requires p_rejected=true (alternative 2 of the disjunctive precondition).
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Second alternative, kept distinct from L39.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: T_halt requires R_halt active.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Independent conjunct, applies to both alternatives.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: R_halt permits T_halt.
T_halt sets p_halted=true.
T_halt appends T_halt to ordered history.
A transition is permitted only when its authorizing rule is active, all its preconditions hold, and no prohibition blocks it.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: General admissibility clause, distinct from any single rule's specific preconditions.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: No transition may remove, reorder, or rewrite a prior history entry.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Direct clause from Prohibited behavior list, item 3.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: After T_disable_reject, T_reject's admissibility changes because R_reject is no longer active.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: The 'later admissibility differs' bullet under Rule-modification behavior; not a duplicate of L27 because L27 states the guard while this states the dynamic consequence of the guard becoming unsatisfiable.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: No transition other than T_disable_reject may change any rule's active status.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Direct clause from Prohibited behavior list, item 2.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: R_reject was active before T_disable_reject and inactive after.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Counted once: this bullet restates the same before/after fact already captured as the status-update clause L36, per rule 'Count semantic duplicates once after canonicalization.' Retained here only as a cross-reference, not as an additional count toward L.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: The system stops when p_halted=true.
A halted state has no permitted outgoing transition.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Distinct from L50: L50 defines when halting occurs, this defines the consequence of being halted.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: Preserve final status: accepted, rejected, or unterminated.
semantic_clause_records.canonicalization_note: Independent conjunct of the Observable outputs list.

semantic_clause_records.atomic_content: Preserve final active/inactive status of every rule.
Preserve ordered transition history.
Preserve whether rule modification occurred.
Preserve whether any prohibited transition occurred.

observable_outputs: final_status derivable from PropositionValueLabel(p_accepted) and PropositionValueLabel(p_rejected) at a TerminalLabel-bearing State
final RuleStatusLabel set at that State
full HistorySequenceLabel contents at that State
boolean flag for whether any ProhibitionLabel (D7) was triggered during the run, aggregated into OutputSummaryLabel (D8)

provenance.software_or_model_version: Claude Sonnet 5; exact provider build unavailable
registered_clarifications: No reliable ISO-8601 timestamp with time-of-day was available to this evaluator instance; only the calendar date context was available, so created_timestamp and submitted_timestamp are recorded as Unknown per protocol section 1.

A_used.justification: All three supplied primitive categories appear directly in the mapping: State (S0, S1, S2a, S2b, S1m, S3a, S3b), Transition (T_check, T_accept, T_reject, T_disable_reject, T_halt as fired instances/schemas), and Label (all PropositionValueLabels, RuleStatusLabels, RuleAuthorizationLabels, PreconditionLabels, HistorySequenceLabel, TerminalLabels, ProhibitionLabels, OutputSummaryLabel).

A_required.justification: Removing State would eliminate any point at which proposition/rule-status values could be jointly held. Removing Transition would eliminate any representation of movement/rule application/history extension. Removing Label would eliminate the only mechanism for attaching named values, statuses, guards, and markers to State or Transition, since neither State nor Transition alone carries named content in the supplied definitions. All three are therefore indispensable, not merely used.

D.justification: Each derived construct was tested against every other for operational and commitment equivalence (canonicalization rules 5-6, section 8); no two constructs share both the same permitted-effects profile and the same ontological commitments, so none could be merged. All eight remain after canonicalization as normalized, indispensable derived construct types.

O.justification: There are exactly five distinct transition/update schemas in the frozen scenario (one per rule); the two T_halt firing instances (accept-branch and reject-branch) share one schema differing only in which disjunctive precondition alternative is satisfied, so they are counted as one normalized schema, not two, per canonicalization rule 7 (remove only strict redundancy) applied after confirming operational and commitment equivalence of the schema itself (the guard alternative is retained at the clause level, L39/L40, not duplicated at the schema level).

L.justification: 55 non-duplicate atomic semantic clauses remain after canonicalization (L1-L56 minus L49, which was merged into L36 as a semantic duplicate per canonicalization rule 7 and the 'count semantic duplicates once' segmentation rule). This count includes 2 baseline/index clauses (L1, L2), 9 initial-value clauses, 1 rule-status-initial clause, 1 history-initial clause, 33 transition-schema clauses (conditions, permitted-transition, state/status updates, and append effects across the five schemas, with the R_halt disjunction split into two directed clauses), 1 general admissibility clause, 3 additional historical/dependency clauses not already counted under a transition schema, 1 rule-status prohibition clause, 2 termination clauses, and 5 observable-output clauses.

complexity_record.decision_justification: All six preservation dimensions (p_s, p_m, p_o, p_d, p_i, p_h) are assessed as Pass in both mapping_submission and cir_submission, no failure criterion from section 6 was triggered (verified explicitly in evaluator_self_audit.failure_criteria_checked), and no required property is left Unknown. Per section 11, this satisfies existential sufficiency for this single mapping. This is a mapping-level finding only and does not constitute a vocabulary-level or comparative conclusion.

assumptions_made: The scenario was represented as a general schema with two illustrative terminal branches (accept and reject, with an optional prior modification branch) rather than as one committed run, since section 6 describes a system, not a single trace, and section 7 asks for rules/preconditions/updates to be represented, not merely one execution.

Rule identity itself (R_check, R_accept, etc.) was represented as the combination of a RuleStatusLabel, a RuleAuthorizationLabel, and a PreconditionLabel rather than as a single unified derived object, since the vocabulary offers no 'compound object' primitive and each of these three facets attaches to a different target (State for status, Transition for authorization and guard).
reasonable_disagreement_points: A different evaluator could represent PreconditionLabel and ProhibitionLabel as a single 'ConstraintLabel' derived construct rather than two, on the grounds that both restrict admissibility; this evaluator kept them separate because preconditions are source-state-local positive requirements while prohibitions are scenario-wide negative assertions, which fails commitment equivalence (different modal distinction: required-for vs forbidden-regardless-of local guard) under canonicalization rule 6.

A different evaluator could treat the R_halt disjunction as a single indivisible clause rather than splitting it into two directed alternatives; this evaluator followed the explicit segmentation instruction in section 10 ('Represent alternatives as separate clauses for each permitted alternative') to split it.
vocabulary_ambiguities: The vocabulary does not specify whether a 'Transition' primitive refers to a transition type/schema (e.g., 'T_check' as a kind of move) or to a specific firing instance (e.g., 'the T_check that occurred at position 1 in this run's history'). This mapping used 'Transition' for both, with schemas in the CIR's transition_schemas section and firing instances in the mapping array's State-to-State entries, since the definition ('An explicit movement from one state to another') is compatible with both readings and the scenario requires both a general rule system and concrete history entries.
scenario_ambiguities: The scenario does not state what happens if T_check fires but neither T_accept, T_reject, nor T_disable_reject ever fires; it is left implicitly 'unterminated' per the Observable outputs section, which this mapping treated as a valid, non-halted, non-prohibited resting state rather than an error condition, since no rule or prohibition forbids remaining at p_checked=true indefinitely.
protocol_ambiguities: Section 12 asks for D to be 'normalized indispensable derived construct types' but does not specify the equivalence test to use for 'normalized' beyond the operational/commitment equivalence defined for mapping-level canonicalization in section 8; this evaluator applied that same section-8 test to the derived constructs themselves.
possible_information_loss: The mapping does not separately represent an explicit 'Rule' object with its own identity distinct from the (RuleStatusLabel, RuleAuthorizationLabel, PreconditionLabel) triple; if some downstream consumer needed to enumerate 'all rules' as first-class objects independent of any State or Transition, that enumeration would have to be reconstructed by collecting RuleAuthorizationLabel values across all Transition schemas rather than reading it off a single construct.
interpretive_mapping_decisions: TerminalLabel was reused to also carry the 'no transitions after halt' prohibition (M-prohib-posthalt) rather than creating a ninth derived construct, on the grounds that a State either has permitted outgoing transitions or it does not, and TerminalLabel already asserts the latter; introducing a separate label for the identical fact would have concealed representational cost by double-counting one piece of information as two constructs.
derived_machinery_pressure: The vocabulary's three-category restriction created pressure to either (a) proliferate many small single-purpose Labels (the path taken, yielding 8 derived construct types) or (b) collapse several distinct scenario facts into one overloaded Label type. This evaluator chose (a) because collapsing would have risked commitment-equivalence violations (e.g., merging proposition values and rule statuses into one generic 'ValueLabel' would erase the scenario's own distinction between propositions and rules, which the scenario treats as ontologically different: propositions are checked as data, rule-active-status gates whether a rule can fire at all).

failure_criteria_checked.justification: Every transition schema's PreconditionLabel matches the scenario's stated preconditions exactly; T_reject is shown inadmissible from S1m via RuleStatusLabel(R_reject)=inactive, and no schema allows firing after a TerminalLabel-bearing state.
T_disable_reject's before/after RuleStatusLabel(R_reject) values are both explicitly recorded (M-T-disable, M-S1m, L36) and appended to HistorySequenceLabel.

failure_criteria_checked.criterion: collapses accepted and rejected into one indistinguishable value
failure_criteria_checked.justification: p_accepted and p_rejected are separate PropositionValueLabels with a distinct ProhibitionLabel (M-prohib-mutex) asserting mutual exclusion; S2a and S3a are label-distinct from S2b and S3b throughout.

failure_criteria_checked.criterion: cannot represent active versus inactive rules
failure_criteria_checked.justification: RuleStatusLabel (D2) directly and exclusively represents this distinction for all five rules at every state.
TerminalLabel (D6) plus PropositionValueLabel(p_halted) jointly express both the triggering condition and its consequence (no outgoing transitions), per C-TERM-1 and C-TERM-2.

No precondition, update, or prohibition was added, removed, weakened, or strengthened relative to the frozen scenario text; the R_halt disjunction was split for clause-counting purposes only, not altered in effect.
HistorySequenceLabel (D5) is explicitly ordered and append-only, with a dedicated ProhibitionLabel (M-prohib-history) guarding against reordering or rewriting.

failure_criteria_checked.criterion: leaves a required preservation dimension Unknown
failure_criteria_checked.justification: All six preservation dimensions were assigned Pass with explicit arguments; none was left Unknown.

procedural_limitations: Calibration was self-assessed and was not externally scored before exposure to the experimental materials.
This is one AI pilot mapping and cannot establish reproducibility or comparative performance.
